scope: S4096 layer-2 sparse-MLA backward full-boundary gate with selected-slot query-major dQ kept intact and union/group-aware dKV applied only to dKV

contract.dQ: selected-slot query-major QK -> P -> scaled_dP -> dQ path remains outside the compressed-group union operand axis
contract.dKV: union/group-aware dKV-only path uses forward-known token x union-group selected-slot sidecar, then atomically accumulates reduced dKV
contract.measurement: two-launch measured boundary: selected-slot query-major dQ/no-dKV followed by union dKV-only
contract.not_final_fusion: This does not yet share QK/P/scaled_dP intermediates between dQ and dKV; it measures whether the dKV epilogue gain survives when paired with the intact dQ owner.

read.best_result: The sharper selected-dQ plus union-dKV split boundary is correct but still slower than the retained Triton full sparse-attention backward: 12.21 ms versus 11.13 ms.
read.why_the_sum_was_misleading: The derived sum of matched single-token no-dKV plus union dKV-only predicted about 10.24 ms, but the measured two-launch boundary is 12.21 ms. The extra overhead means the dKV slice win does not materialize unless dQ and dKV share the source-boundary producer/intermediates inside one fused owner.
read.union_full_rejected: Letting union rows serve dQ remains rejected: even the best nearby block_u=256 full union dQ+dKV is 20.14 ms, and smaller block_u settings were worse.
read.next_kernel_contract: Keep selected-slot query-major QK/P/scaled_dP/dQ. Add a dKV epilogue that consumes the same P/scaled_dP while still hot, performs group-aware local reduction before global atomics, and avoids a second source-boundary recompute/launch.

decision.reason: No full sparse-attention latency uplift was materialized; best correct measured full-boundary split is 12.21 ms, slower than the retained 11.13 ms gate.
